title: Goldman may be trying to bail on Apple Card
text: Four years after partnering with Apple on the launch of the Apple Card, Goldman Sachs may be eyeing the exits. The Wall Street Journal reports that Goldman is “looking for a way out” of its high-profile deal with Apple, which recently expanded to include savings accounts for Apple Card holders.

title: Goldman in talks to offload Apple credit, savings products to American Express, source says
text: Goldman Sachs is in talks to offload its Apple credit card and high-yield savings account products to American Express, a source told CNBC's Leslie Picker.

title: Goldman Sachs is looking to leave Apple partnership: WSJ
text: Goldman Sachs Group Inc. GS, -0.17% is weighing whether to leave its partnership with Apple Inc. AAPL, +2.31%, amid a broader retreat from consumer banking, the Wall Street Journal reported on Friday. The Journal, citing people familiar with the matter, said Goldman was seeking ways to hand off its Apple credit card and other initiatives from the partnership to American Express Co. AXP, +1.23%.

title: Goldman Sachs Is Still A High Quality Buy
text: Goldman Sachs has strong fundamentals, attractive valuation, and growth opportunities, particularly in its wealth management and alternative assets segments. There are risks, including competition and the impact of a possible economic downturn on the demand for financial services. Despite these risks, I believe Goldman Sachs is a good investment due to its stable operation, sales and EPS growth, and its position as a trusted financial institution making shares of Goldman Sachs a buy.

title: Russell 2000 small-cap index could outperform S&P 500 in next year, Goldman Sachs analysts say
text: The Russell 2000 small-cap index could outperform the S&P 500 over the next 12 months, according to analysts at Goldman Sachs (NYSE:GS). In a report published Wednesday, Goldman analysts wrote that the Russell 2000 should gain 14% over the next year while projecting a 9% gain for the S&P 500 for the same period.

title: The Fed is about to reveal how safe America's biggest banks are after the crisis earlier this year
text: The Federal Reserve is set to release the results from its annual bank stress test on Wednesday after US markets close. The tests, born out of the Great Recession, are undertaken in order to determine if the nation's largest banks can withstand a severe economic downturn.

title: Odey Asset Management suspends flagship fund
text: Scandal-hit Odey Asset Management has suspended its main fund amid a tidal wave of requests from investors looking to pull money from the firm. “The fund board has determined that it would not be in the best interests of the fund and its shareholders as a whole to continue to permit issues and redemptions of shares,” the firm told investors on Tuesday.

title: Goldman Sachs Looks Interesting, Yielding 3.18%, Going Into The Second Half Of 2023
text: Goldman Sachs shares have declined by 25.01% since August 2021 and are currently trading at a 3.19% premium to book value and 9.81 times 2023 projected earnings. GS has a strong history of rewarding shareholders through buybacks and increasing dividends, with an 11-year streak of annual dividend increases and a 25.07% reduction in shares outstanding over the past decade. The company's future prospects look promising, with potential for increased revenue from brokerage commissions, trading and principal transactions, and asset management fees, as well as benefiting from IPO and M&A activity.

title: Goldman Sachs stock falls amid reports of big writedown on CEO's GreenSky deal: report
text: Goldman Sachs (NYSE:GS) faces a significant writedown on its investment in GreenSky as it seeks buyers for the fintech lender, CNBC reported on Friday.  Its shares fell 1.5% to $314.88 shortly after the market opened.

title: Goldman Sachs may book write-down in the value of GreenSky as sales process continues: CNBC
text: Synchrony Financial SYF, -1.07%, KKR & Co. KKR, -2.54%, Apollo Global Management APO, -1.28%, Sixth Street Partners and Warburg Pincus have bid on the installment loan business of Goldman Sachs Group Inc.'s GS, -1.25% GreenSky consumer lending business, but the bids are coming up below what Goldman wants, CNBC reported, citing people familiar with the process. Goldman Sachs may end up getting far less for the GreenSky businesses than it paid when it acquired the company for $1.7 billion in stock when the deal closed in 2021, the report said.

title: Goldman Sachs faces big writedown on GreenSky deal, CNBC reports

title: Limble Announces $58M Series B Funding Round Led by Goldman Sachs Asset Management, Bringing Total Valuation to $450M
text: Funds will support multi-product roadmap to capitalize on growing demand for modern CMMS LEHI, Utah , June 22, 2023 /PRNewswire/ -- Limble ,  the leading provider of modern computerized maintenance management systems (CMMS), announced today a $58M Series B funding round led by the Growth Equity business within Goldman Sachs Asset Management (Goldman Sachs), bringing the company's valuation to $450M. The funding will be used to expand Limble's product portfolio, further develop its powerful and intuitive user interface for asset monitoring and maintenance and accelerate its go-to-market strategy.

title: Second Goldman Sachs veteran joins private credit firm Star Mountain
text: Star Mountain Capital LLC, a private loan provider with $3.5 billion of assets under management, on Wednesday said Goldman Sachs Group Inc. GS, -2.24% vice president MaryAnn Pasquarello Stauffer has joined the firm as a managing director in its Philadelphia office. It's the second Goldman Sachs executive to join Star Mountain this year after ex-Goldman partner George Mattson became president of the firm.

title: Insider trading: Understanding the murky white collar criminal world
text: Insider trading—the act of trading a company's stock or other securities by individuals with access to non-public, material information about the company—has long been the white-collar crime equivalent of a magic trick: often suspected, rarely proven. A US court case involving a former Goldman Sachs banker and his squash partner has catapulted the issue back into the headlines as well as underlining the need to combat the issue once and for all.

title: Kodiak Gas Services launches up to $352M IPO
text: Kodiak Gas Services said on Tuesday that it intends to raise up to $352 million in an initial public offering (IPO) in the United States. The operator of contract compression infrastructure said it plans to sell 16 million shares of its stock at an anticipated IPO price of between $19 and $22 per share, raising up to $352 million.

title: Goldman Sachs stock falls into the red on WSJ report of SEC probe into the bank's role in securities sales prior to Silicon Valley Bank failure
text: Goldman Sachs Group Inc. GS stock is down 0.5%, reversing gains from earlier in the session, after The Wall Street Journal reported that the Securities and Exchange Commission is investigating the role of the bank in buying Silicon Valley Bank's securities portfolio prior to the bank's collapse in March. Citing people familiar with the probe, the newspaper reported that the Justice Department has also issued a subpoena to Goldman as part of its broader investigation of Silicon Valley Bank.

title: Fed, SEC probing Goldman Sachs's role in Silicon Valley Bank's final days - WSJ
text: The U.S. Federal Reserve and the Securities and Exchange Commission are investigating Goldman Sachs Group's role in buying Silicon Valley Bank's securities portfolio while it was working on the bank's capital raise, the Wall Street Journal reported on Thursday.

title: Goldman Sachs to intensify cost-cutting drive amid continued revenue concerns
text: Goldman Sachs (NYSE:GS) management is set to take its cost-cutting measures to the next level as part of its bid to slash $1 billion in costs, including potential further job cuts. According to a Reuters report citing four sources familiar with the matter, the company's managing directors met this month and were told to accelerate their cost-cutting efforts.

title: Banks report the largest-ever quarterly sequential drop in uninsured deposits in Q1: S&P
text: Bank of America Corp. BAC, Citigroup Inc. C and Wells Fargo & Co. WFC reported drops in uninsured deposits as of March 31 as part of an overall sequential decline of 7.8% or $597.49 billion in uninsured deposits by all U.S. banks as of the end of the first quarter, S&P Global Inc. said in a research note published Thursday. JPMorgan Chase & Co. JPM reported a 1.9% increase in uninsured deposits.

title: Goldman Sachs COO warns of 'mini stagflationary' scenario where sticky inflation eats away at economic growth
text: Brace for a "mini stagflation" scenario where sticky inflation eats away at US economic growth, Goldman Sachs' COO said.  "That's not going to be called a recession, but it's not going to feel great," John Waldron said at the Bloomberg Invest conference.

title: Goldman Sachs COO claims US faces "mini stagflation"
text: The US may yet avoid a recession but still faces the possibility of “mini stagflation,” Goldman Sachs Group Inc President and Chief Operating Officer John Waldron said at the Bloomberg Invest conference. “This is the best predicted recession that hasn't happened yet and may not happen,” Waldron said.

title: Odey Asset Management reportedly faces losing access to key brokers
text: The broking operations of Goldman Sachs (NYSE:GS), JPMorgan, and Morgan Stanley (NYSE:MS), are reassessing their relationships with Odey Asset Management amid sexual misconduct allegations against the firm's founder, Crispin Odey, Reuters has reported, citing sources familiar with the matter. The allegations, stemming from a joint investigation by the Financial Times and Tortoise Media, include accusations from 13 women over a span of 25 years.

title: Goldman Sachs Asset Management buying Norwegian salmon farming company Frøy
text: A unit of Goldman Sachs Group Inc.'s GS, +0.81% alternative investing division Goldman Sachs Asset Management on Monday said it would acquire Frøy ASA FROY, +0.54% in a deal that values the salmon farming transportation company at about $595 million. Falcon Bidco AS, a company owned by infrastructure funds managed by Goldman Sachs Asset Management (GASM), said it would pay seller NTS AS, a unit of SalMAR ASA SALM, -2.96% SALRF, +5.25%, the equivalent of about $6.90 a share in local currency for its 72% stake in Frøy, plus it is also making a mandatory offer to acquire the remaining shares of the company.

title: Banks reportedly could face 20% hike to capital rules after string of failures
text: The Journal said that the precise amount of capital requirements will depend on the bank's business, with US megabanks with big trading businesses expected to face the largest increases.

title: Goldman Sachs Asset Management agrees to acquire 72.11% of the shares in Frøy at NOK 76.50 per share and launch subsequent mandatory offer
text: Frøya, 5 June 2023 Falcon Bidco AS (the “Offeror”), a company indirectly wholly owned by infrastructure funds managed by Goldman Sachs Asset Management, has entered into an agreement (the “Transaction Agreement”) with NTS AS (“NTS”), a wholly owned subsidiary of SalMar ASA (“SalMar”) (OSE ticker: SALM), and Frøy ASA (“Frøy” or the “Company”) (OSE ticker: FROY), whereby the Offeror on certain terms and conditions will acquire NTS' entire ownership stake in Frøy, representing approximately 72.11% of the shares in Frøy (the “Share Sale”), and following completion of the Share Sale make an unconditional mandatory cash offer to acquire all other shares in Frøy (the “Mandatory Offer”, and together with the Share Sale, the “Transaction”). The Transaction is the result of the strategic review related to the Company announced on 13 January 2023 (the “Strategic Review”), with focus on maximizing value for the shareholders in Frøy.
